payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay, depending on how many rounds you guys want, that'll put you down about two gold probably by the time the night is done.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Are my ears tucked back in?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: The ears are in and make sure that the mask is secure. Have fun.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You're really all right, Caleb?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm okay. It was a rough night.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I know, but you went a bit catatonic.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Did you see anything or have a vision? I don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No, I did not see a vision. I saw a burning person.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Anybody who tries to buy me a drink, I slip a silver into their pocket. Let me know how low we get.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. They're common folk for the most part. Nobody seems to notice. Those that even do watch you aren't going to argue. Now, is that just drinks on you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: When any time we let them buy us drinks, I'm just going to put a silver in anybody's pocket who's buying us drinks.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Crute, while he has been happy to provide you the evenings free for two rooms, even for all you've done, "I am a businessman, through and through, so I'll give you a discount. Three silver."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As the evening continues, some folks come up and tell their stories to you, give you thanks. Share a drink if someone was lost. Some recovered, some not. Overall, everyone seems to be very appreciative. Eventually, whether through just general exhaustion or imbibement, you bring yourself to rest, finding yourselves back to the singular room you've all been sharing here at the Feed and Mead.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh yeah. Should we get another room now that we've got some extra coin?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How many rooms do they have at the inn?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We can get an extra room.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'll take my own room if y'all don't want in.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: But we have always been roommates.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Well, it's a heavy pouch and contains about 113 gold pieces.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do I know what the Evening Nip is? Do I recognize that?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You don't know the old evening nip? (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I finish reading everything, I go over to a sleeping Jester, pull her blankets up and tuck her in a little bit, and then I crash out.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Two quick things. One, I'd like to ask Caleb, when he's done with his insanely long ritual: That pouch that I gave you, what was in it?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Did you give me a pouch?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I gave you a pouch before. You might not have felt it, but it's in your back pocket.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, what is in it? Let's see.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, Shakäste's pouch. You find what is roughly a handful of bird seed.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Wouldn't it be funny if there was, like, two copper and a bunch of bird seed?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay, yeah. Looking about, at this point in the evening most of it is filtering out. The few stragglers there are the older folks, grizzled farmhands with sunburned skin, thick fingers that have seen a lot of field work, a lot of them reminiscing about days of old, some of them talking about some folks they know that were lost over the previous events, but they seem drunk enough to at least be smiling and laughing about these old stories they bring up. You continue to study these personalities, I imagine you're probably taking in quirks, taking in elements of their body language, their vocal intonations for your own purposes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, make a general strength check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The pearl pops out and flies across the room, never to be found again.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 15? It doesn't take you too long. Working on it back and forth you eventually have enough give where the four claws that hold the pearl into the ring set eventually give way and it pops out. It does hit the ground and roll and you quickly catch up to it and grab it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I take what's left of the ring and I throw it across the room for Frumpkin to go chasing it across.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Frumpkin just watches it land. (thunk, thunk)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: While everybody is going up to their rooms, can I stay down-- are you staying down?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I'm going upstairs, I'm going to pass out on the floor.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll stay downstairs and do some people-watching. Call it character study.

payload.raw_text: Okay. As you all eventually close the evening, the morning sun comes to, with Nott sleeping in a little further after staying up late creating a vial of acid.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns_last5.text: Oh, I'm going upstairs, I'm going to pass out on the floor.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: I'll stay downstairs and do some people-watching. Call it character study.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Okay, yeah. Looking about, at this point in the evening most of it is filtering out. The few stragglers there are the older folks, grizzled farmhands with sunburned skin, thick fingers that have seen a lot of field work, a lot of them reminiscing about days of old, some of them talking about some folks they know that were lost over the previous events, but they seem drunk enough to at least be smiling and laughing about these old stories they bring up. You continue to study these personalities, I imagine you're probably taking in quirks, taking in elements of their body language, their vocal intonations for your own purposes.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It's been probably, at this point, four days, would be my guess.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And they're wanting an extract, so--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: That means it would be extra strong, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Are the Baumbauchs trying to poison people?!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my gosh! Well, the Gentleman wants to poison people. Oh, no, the Baumbauchs, yeah, I understand. Wow, do we need to tell someone about this?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No. Just keep it in the back of your head if you hear something, if something spikes your interest--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: If we hear about an outbreak of people vomiting--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I want to know about these evening nipples-- evening nip.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: In the coming week! That's soon. When did you steal this?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How many days has it been?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You guys all wake up and get your breakfast situated and sorted, Nott joining you a little later on, but you are fed, the morning is fresh, what would you like to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No. Just keep it in the back of your head if you hear something, if something spikes your interest--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: If we hear about an outbreak of people vomiting--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I want to know about these evening nipples-- evening nip.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: In the coming week! That's soon. When did you steal this?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How many days has it been?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's been probably, at this point, four days, would be my guess.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my gosh, what if we could intercept it? Then we could have all of the poison, and the nips.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Let's keep this-- we'll workshop it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah we did, we definitely did.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh man, I'm really hungry, though, I'm going to go downstairs.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Counting through, they finish each bit counted. "That comes to--" They pull up a small chest and begin to count out change into a large sack. Then that fills up into a secondary sack, into a third one, finishing with, "A total of 570 gold pieces."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, it was 19 sets, I marked down sets. Technically Matt, I'm not telling Bryce this, five of those sets are hyenas. 14 of those are gnolls for sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Bryce has a hard time telling the difference at the moment, having been overworked and stressed with the recent goings-on.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Seven dead bodies at five gold per seven, that will come to 35 gold pieces as well." Which they fill out for you. "Then the live ones were how many?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, it was 19 sets, I marked down sets. Technically Matt, I'm not telling Bryce this, five of those sets are hyenas. 14 of those are gnolls for sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Bryce has a hard time telling the difference at the moment, having been overworked and stressed with the recent goings-on.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Counting through, they finish each bit counted. "That comes to--" They pull up a small chest and begin to count out change into a large sack. Then that fills up into a secondary sack, into a third one, finishing with, "A total of 570 gold pieces."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We also brought home live people and also some dead people. We brought home seven dead bodies.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Interesting, well that's much larger for the tastes of most field folk out here. I lived there for about three years when I was younger, in the West Outersteads before joining the Crownsguard and shipping off to Bladegarden. If you are indeed off to Zadash and are in need of a friend, if she's still there. Ask for Claudia Sheed at the Leaky Tap."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Now we come to the next word. N-E-I-N.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I feel like I was part of something crowd sourced similar to this." I feel like I've been part of a game call "Mighty Number Nine."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. It's a game that I-- It's a Mega Man--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes, I know. Oh that game. Anyway!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "All right, The Mighty Nein, that is on the paperwork. Are you staying a while in Alfield then?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are we staying here? I mean, do you want to keep hiring us for always?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I don't have much in the way of work here for your type at the moment."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You seem to be at peace with the current situation, I think it might be in our best interest that we moved on.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Certain, fine. She's got a good head on her shoulders and at the very least she may be able to guide you to anything you're looking for. Hopefully. Oh! I almost forgot, follow me please." They stand up and close the ledger and lead you outside. About two blocks down by the outside of the stables, titled the On The Wind Stables. There you see outside a hardy looking female dragonborn. Rotund and strong, no tail, who is black scaled, turns around as you approach and gives a quick hand up to Bryce and Bryce approaches. "Ah, thank you Durmas. Everyone this is Durmas. She is the main stable hand here at the On The Wind. We've taken, as a bit of appreciation here, we've outfitted you with a new cart as the previous one was a bit-- It had carried many of our dead, so Durmas." And Durmas steps forth and says, "Great. I have presented for you this cart that we had made. It belonged to somebody here, but it is now yours. It is well made, it is larger than what you had previously and does not smell of blood and feces."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "At the Leaky Tap at the East Outersteads."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I should ask you, is that like the big inn, the big place?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What would be the biggest inn in Zadash? Like the biggest place that if you go like, "Oh, I need to find the biggest inn in Zadash," this is what it is?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That would be the The Pillow Trove. That's in Tri-Spires. I don't know if you can afford that? I don't know, maybe you can! I don't know."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I just need the most popular one, because that's where my mom is going be sending me stuff. The Pillow Trove.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The Pillow Trove. But The Leaky Tap I recommend. Claudia's great; she owns the place if she's still there."

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "I'll return in the next 15 minutes." Starosta turns and gives a long look of simmering fury behind a forced smile to Jester. Spins around then walks off in a haughty march to wherever he came from.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: The Ruby of The Sea says hello.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You watch as his face loses color. His hand drops to his side. "Um. I will be happy to write you a letter of recommendation, but I cannot assure you that it will be accepted as there are many points of acceptance within the Academy. And is quite a bit of travel to Rexxentrum anyway. Is this to the actual Soltryce?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: If you wouldn't mind, that would be most kind of you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Very well, it will be done in just a moment. What is your name?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Make sure he spells it right, Fjord.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Just been sitting on that shit, haven't you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We're not going to make it through all of them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I was keeping it together so well. Here, buddy.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. This makes sense for you since you grew up in the Menagerie Coast, though in Port Damali. The Ruby of the Sea is well known in some circles. You've only heard rumors. Belongs to an establishment that specializes in all forms of socializing, entertainment, and is one of the most prominent brothels in the Menagerie Coast, in which the Ruby of the Sea is known to be the star courtesan. Many travel all across the Menagerie Coast and beyond, supposedly, to be within her company. Many have fallen in love with her and had their hearts broken. There are poems and songs that have wandered through bars and taverns that spoke of her, that first caught your interest. It seems like a faint, distant tale that was just a myth that people made up or someone spoke of. Ruby of the Sea, it sounds amorphous.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I was keeping it together so well. Here, buddy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'll return in the next 15 minutes." Starosta turns and gives a long look of simmering fury behind a forced smile to Jester. Spins around then walks off in a haughty march to wherever he came from.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He really should have changed his hair, I mean it's pretty hideous.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You may be my new hero.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How is it that you saw him before?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, he visited my mother years ago.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And your mother's The Ruby of The Sea?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yes, The Ruby of The Sea. You've heard of her?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I have now. Have I heard of her?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Don't fuck me. That's a natural one.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I don't get a bonus, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay, no that's fine. Oh, I wouldn't have heard?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No. It's outside of the Empire.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: He huffs off, they all begin to leave as you guys go to your cart. The Watchmaster comes up one last time and they say, "Sincerely, you've saved many lives and... A paltry sum I could get approved, but know that you have my gratitude."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Your father is your father, how could you not be curious about your father?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I know, right? I mean, I've never met him, I thought it would be cool. That's who I was looking for when I met Fjord, over there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: But it is still your right to know your parents. Who they are, you have to understand where you came from.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I mean, he's supposed to be pretty darn cool. He wears lots of rings on his fingers too, you guys!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Ahem!" You look and see that the Crownsguard are approaching. And there's the Starosta Kosh there with a rolled up piece of parchment which he presents with a broad smile. "As you requested."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Of course, it's everything I can do. Now, be on your way."

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: It's getting towards the start of fall.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well I will tell you, we'll get to that, but I think today we call your birthday.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you know, we never talked about that. I didn't realize that, so today is your Geburtstag, today is your birthday.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: He doesn't know. He will tell us later.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. Well for right now, let's say it's Thursday.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Every year on a Thursday it is your birthday.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: This is a very special occasion.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you know a birthday song too?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No. I'm sorry. But I do have this pickle in my bag.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It was sitting in the jar and I put it in my bag because we put the ears in here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Congratulations, today you are a mighty nine.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Exactly. Speaking of which, guys. You've begun your trek northward back on the Amber Road, passing through the high grass fields, the rolling hills. The sky grows more cloudy, and as the day progresses, uneventful, three horses carrying crownsguard are patrolling down the Amber Road and pass by, and watch them nod as they continue on. As you continue down, for the remainder of the day-- you guys probably left around noon. Eight hours of travel, would say is a standard travel day for a horse, so that'll be just past sunset.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Hello everyone, and welcome back. Before we hop back in, the two winners of the Wyrmwood giveaway are Jevons and Nicholas Fernandez. Jevons and Nicholas Fernandez, congratulations, you guys won. Danoba will contact you and get your information so we can get that sent out to you, so congrats. All right, everyone seems ready and iced.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: A rib will be pulled when you turn and sneeze.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Exactly. Speaking of which, guys. You've begun your trek northward back on the Amber Road, passing through the high grass fields, the rolling hills. The sky grows more cloudy, and as the day progresses, uneventful, three horses carrying crownsguard are patrolling down the Amber Road and pass by, and watch them nod as they continue on. As you continue down, for the remainder of the day-- you guys probably left around noon. Eight hours of travel, would say is a standard travel day for a horse, so that'll be just past sunset.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Hello everyone, and welcome back. Before we hop back in, the two winners of the Wyrmwood giveaway are Jevons and Nicholas Fernandez. Jevons and Nicholas Fernandez, congratulations, you guys won. Danoba will contact you and get your information so we can get that sent out to you, so congrats. All right, everyone seems ready and iced.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: A rib will be pulled when you turn and sneeze.

payload.raw_text: Keeping an eye out, best you can, the sleep takes you eventually and you end up cheating on the last few hours of your watch. Nevertheless, you all come to consciousness in the morning intact and fine. You begin your way further on the road for a second day. Now, who's guiding the-- I would say more of who's keeping a perimeter watch during the next day's journey?

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns_last5.text: Your mighty nine allows you to keep an eye out as the dark perimeter seems to bring no shapes or movements of interest other than the occasional sound of the wind blowing through the fields and the distant dark. Then your watch is up. Beauregard, perception check, please.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Keeping an eye out, best you can, the sleep takes you eventually and you end up cheating on the last few hours of your watch. Nevertheless, you all come to consciousness in the morning intact and fine. You begin your way further on the road for a second day. Now, who's guiding the-- I would say more of who's keeping a perimeter watch during the next day's journey?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It doesn't make much of a (fut fut), much to your disappointment. But a fire is built. You all begin to bed down for the evening, and who's taking first watch?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You can break it up between, probably let's say, three watches over a casual evening.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. An uneventful evening continues for you. You trade off to Fjord. Fjord, make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Your mighty nine allows you to keep an eye out as the dark perimeter seems to bring no shapes or movements of interest other than the occasional sound of the wind blowing through the fields and the distant dark. Then your watch is up. Beauregard, perception check, please.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Keeping an eye out, best you can, the sleep takes you eventually and you end up cheating on the last few hours of your watch. Nevertheless, you all come to consciousness in the morning intact and fine. You begin your way further on the road for a second day. Now, who's guiding the-- I would say more of who's keeping a perimeter watch during the next day's journey?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It doesn't make much of a (fut fut), much to your disappointment. But a fire is built. You all begin to bed down for the evening, and who's taking first watch?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You can break it up between, probably let's say, three watches over a casual evening.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. An uneventful evening continues for you. You trade off to Fjord. Fjord, make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Your mighty nine allows you to keep an eye out as the dark perimeter seems to bring no shapes or movements of interest other than the occasional sound of the wind blowing through the fields and the distant dark. Then your watch is up. Beauregard, perception check, please.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right. Continuing on for the remainder of the day, seeing nothing else that catches your eye along the journey, eventually the night comes to a close once more. You guys have pretty much hit the extent of your horses' energy for the day's travel right as the sun begins to set. So you can pull off and make another camp for this evening.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are they going the opposite direction than us?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (whispering) Should I ask if they're the Gentlemen? (shouting) Good to see you, Gentlemen!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Gentlemen. Do any of them look--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I mean, they're well-dressed. They look to be of bourgeois class. Not upper crust, but better than most you've encountered on this side of the Empire at least. And they seem to almost-- aside from glaring as you pass-- pay no mind to any of your verbiage beyond, you motherfuckers.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I think they thought we were patronizing them.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. You are sitting back for a minute , the fire crackling to a very diminutive ember. There's just a bit of light slowly flickering off, framing the outer shapes of your various sleeping friends and companions. You don't hear the snapping of twigs because it's subtle enough. A brief tickle hits the back of your head, Caleb, as the thread perimeter is broken.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Should we three take watch since those three took watch last time?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes, DM, I would like to say that, too. If we're ever camping outside, just because Liam is a dumb fuck and will forget it. Every night, Caleb does silver thread around the group.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. So after setting up your alarm--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (singing) It's murder time in the campaign.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you complete your set up for the evening, make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What the fuck?! We can't roll perception for shit.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I shouldn't have touched this die. This die has been nothing but trouble.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah, it gives you advantage. Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I would like to cast Friends on that gentleman.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Make a sleight of hand check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ah, that's very low. That is a five.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As soon as you begin to-- and, correct me if I'm wrong. Friends-- actually, let me see here. Friends is-- no! It's--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Somatic and material. All I did was just do this with my thumb and I put a little bit of rouge on my cheek.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Yeah, so your charisma checks. The spell seems to take an effect.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You'll have to be careful. We are going for help. Medical help. We all have syphilis. Extreme syphilis.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Is this with advantage, right? That's the effect of the spell.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Correct. You went to intercede. I'll allow you to take the hit on that one then. The damage goes through to you automatically.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And the third one is a natural 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. The first is a 11, 12, 13, damn it. Ja.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is that a natural 20 on the--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It was three things of flame go shooting-- Because I did this. (fire noises)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Immediately as that ray is released. That's a 16 to hit?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: As the flames go out, these come in.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As the flames escape, four crossbow bolts two of them are heavy, and two of them are light crossbow.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I was holding my action for that crossbow bolt. For one at him.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I'll allow you to do one of them.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It was three things of flame go shooting-- Because I did this. (fire noises)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Immediately as that ray is released. That's a 16 to hit?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: As the flames go out, these come in.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As the flames escape, four crossbow bolts two of them are heavy, and two of them are light crossbow.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I was holding my action for that crossbow bolt. For one at him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct. You went to intercede. I'll allow you to take the hit on that one then. The damage goes through to you automatically.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Can I Blood Maledict one of them, or no?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: All right, the one on the left, who looks to be a scraggly redhead, probably close to 20 or 21 years old, wins the bout of boulder-parchment-shears.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Now that the leader's dead, who's the de facto leader here? Speak up, be bold!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Two of the ones go, "Me!" They both look at each other and shoot each other down with these horrible glares and go, "No, it's me!" again, at the same time. Both say, "Fuck you!" at the same time. (Groans) And they're just frustrated.

payload.raw_text: Yeah. During your final watch, as people are finishing up their rest, the sun just barely begins to-- or would be cresting over the hills. The slight change in light is the precursor that notifies you of dawn, however, the heavy, dark clouds in the sky and the sudden sound of thunder immediately followed the freezing cold rainfall that begins to spill across the entirety of your campsite. All of you begin to come to consciousness, finishing your long rest, by immediately being drenched in freezing water of a recent storm here on the Marrow Valley fields.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: Please tell me it's less than a ten.

preceding_turns_last5.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns_last5.text: I mean, that's six perception checks in a row that were under ten.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yeah. During your final watch, as people are finishing up their rest, the sun just barely begins to-- or would be cresting over the hills. The slight change in light is the precursor that notifies you of dawn, however, the heavy, dark clouds in the sky and the sudden sound of thunder immediately followed the freezing cold rainfall that begins to spill across the entirety of your campsite. All of you begin to come to consciousness, finishing your long rest, by immediately being drenched in freezing water of a recent storm here on the Marrow Valley fields.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That was some good lying. Oh boy. Your face is fucked up. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I've been asleep face-down in the cart, it feels like. Where are we?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We're in the same exact place, you're not dead.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Did we make it to Zadash?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, Caleb go back to sleep, you're ruining everyone's sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's literally been about ten minutes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, let's fight these fuckers. Where are they?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: They're all dead, they're gone. We took their horses, they're gone.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They were already gone when you passed out. Go back to sleep. Is he always like this?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. Third and final watch?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Please tell me it's less than a ten.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I mean, that's six perception checks in a row that were under ten.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As you guys are waiting, Caleb's returned, and a few minutes later, maybe about ten minutes after, you watch as Molly approaches, tossing this deeply rusted iron helmet in his hand.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (whisper chants) Scrap metal! Scrap metal! Scrap metal!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Like nobody's going to want metal and we're just going to be driving around that busted jalopy with a shitload of metal.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're unable to make out any details of the construction of it or any significance of it, but it's an old helmet. And it looks like a lot of the edges of it appear to have just slowly been taken by the oxidization--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to take it back anyway.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Yep. (sad meow). As you push into the latter half of the afternoon, a few hours before the sun begins to set, you begin cresting a large hill along the road, before you notice a trio of spiky peaks catching your eye, breaking the skyline ahead of you. As you begin to come higher up to the top of that hillside, you can watch these peaks slowly unveil into the rest of the massive skyline of the city of Zadash, breaking the horizon.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Maybe they'll have nicer things. Arts. Culture.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Of course they will. It's where all the money is.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: They have the best of the best in the north, ja.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do they like arts and culture in the Dwendalian Empire?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys continue moving northward again, the rain still pouring as you all maintain warmth best you can, bundling up with the various travel supplies, blankets and insulation you can muster off the cart and your own--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I was taking in the rain, yo! I like it!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Hard to tell what motions you're doing.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Shut up! I could see it in my head!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Frumpkin is fucking miserable because he's drenched and he does not like to be wet.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: He takes the edges and daintily holds the cloak open presentery style and he's dressed in very fine clothing, like a nice coat that's buttoned, with a little cravat and fine trousers. Or at least they were at one point, the edges are a little tattered. It looks like his one nice suit that he's had for maybe 20 years and it's definitely seen some rough days. You get the sense, from this whole package here, that either he feigns some level of prominence or has fallen very hard from it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Should we go to this dingy tunnel?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You say that because it's a good idea, or because you're feeling--?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't know, he just seems like a good guy some-- I mean, shifty. But I don't think he's trying to fuck us right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: This is a game of trust.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: This is our first chain whisper.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: What is going on right now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Or, not. You're welcome to wander as you see fit but I was just merely willing to help."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What do you have under that cloak? Is it weapons?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "And, looking up at the sky, it appears the sun has set, which means my purchased day is done. It's been an absolute pleasure."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Lot of people come through town?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "A lot of people come through town."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Is it easy to work around the Empire here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "It can be. You can look for work. You can head to the--" He points out here. This would be, "The Signet Wall, if you're more of a sellsword."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The Signet Wall. That's where the military stays, and most of the Crownsguard are holed up."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do you know a good blacksmith?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "The Pentamarket would be a place where you could find. There is the Spark Hammer Smithing, that would be of aid to you. There's also Packwearer's Provisions, but they're more of a smaller scale, smithing goods, tools, and such."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I think we learned a lot.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As you fight it, a moment passes and you see before you for the first time since you've met, a sparkly clean Caleb.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I know of them. This is my first encounter.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Hello. Wow. Look at those horns. I'm so sorry. You know, I have never-- Forget it. I am looking for-- I have been in the woods for so long, I'm so happy to be in a metropolis again. I need ink.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Before we can get to that, I just have to say you're, respectfully, terribly filthy. And this is an establishment that I have to insist requires some more cleanliness." As he waves his hand in front of you, you watch as all the dirt on your coat just begins to sift away. Your clothing becomes clean right up the front of you reaching up towards your neck. The dirt begins to fleck away across your face.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm tilting. I'm arching my face.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "Well, let's go ahead and clear off." He takes the vials off the counter and they set a space, and the three of them sit around. You hear this voice behind this thick curtain go, "Is anybody able to help me here for a minute?" "Right there. The two of you, continue this." One of them goes in the back room to help the fourth one.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Can I have one? I'll have two of those potions, if you don't mind.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Is there a discount for two?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm sorry, but this is a business. No discounts, but we're happy to sell for the price of 50 gold apiece."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do you guys deal in magical weapons here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "We deal with all sorts of enchanted items." He turns as one of the other guys over there goes, "I've got it. We have all manner of objects that we've crafted, enchanted, and designed."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Would you like to buy this?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, are you looking to sell or trade?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It depends on how much you want to pay for it.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "I'll tell you what. If you give me this here majestic weapon, with the ribbon included, and an additional 200 gold pieces on top of that trade, I can just go ahead--" and the other guy who's wandered away goes, "We can take this here fantastic haversack off the wall and make it yours."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You said it needs a little decoration. I take the ribbon off my horn and I wrap it around the handle and I tie it on. What about now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "That's adorable. And I'm not going to lie, I like it better." (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "302 gold. But for trade, I'll probably be able to muster a little more. What are you looking for?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We are looking for something that can hold a lot of doughnuts in it, and also maybe some other things that are heavy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Let me have a look here and see what we can muster. You're looking for something that could-- you traveling type? Is that right?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes. Like a briefcase of carrying.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: A cheat code, if you will.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "There's plenty of vendors. With the raining outside and so late, probably hard to find pastries tonight, but fresh in the morning, just let your nose carry you around. The Pentamarket has plenty."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'd really like, you know, pretty. I'm going to be carrying it, I'd like it to match my outfit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (Arcane incantation) He finishes and he watches the haversack suddenly shimmers past, and the dull, general, brown leather turns to a bright, vibrant pink.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ah! It's beautiful! 200 gold, here you go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You hand it to the one guy, who accepts it. "All right, good. I hope you enjoy it. I'm going to get back to work. Keep it up, me." He goes in the back room. He takes the glaive off. The other guy says, "All right, thank you very much. Any other business you require?"

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You need some pastries, but we'd need somewhere else.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We'll go somewhere else for that. I doubt you guys sell pastries.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Winter's Crest is actually only celebrated in Tal'Dorei. It does not have cultural significance here in Wildemount. I believe you are three to four months out from it. I'll have to check the calendar. You guys are just now cresting into the beginning of fall.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No worries. Just listen for the clanging and the sparks. All right, if that's all, I wish you all adieu, come back any time. We're happy to sell, buy, trade, and generally help out anyone who has money."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Man, I want to learn that trick real bad.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No, where you make real versions of yourself that you get to hang out with!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Did we find mulled wine? Did we find a wine vendor?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh yeah. No, especially with it so cold outside, you can see there were two different carts that had small, pot-heated, basic alcohol and ciders.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: If there were any little confectionaries with that too, we would have bought whatever they had left.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Also, where in the year are we in relation to Winter's Crest?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: You may have to come back and buy more tarts in the morning.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As you guys step outside, you are immediately met by Beauregard and Molly, who have come back with some small wooden cups filled with heated, steaming mulled wine and a small care package of baked goods. Which would mark you guys off at about, I'd say total of six silver for the bunch.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Why do you need them? I like them.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I cast spells with them. I need just a couple.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: But I like to eat them.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (Napoleon Dynamite voice) Jester, give me your tarts!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How many tarts do I have?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: I was going to say, because you guys got to get going pretty soon. What we can do is we can say at this point in the evening, it looks like the blacksmithy is currently closed off to any sort of wares-selling. They're in the process of creation. You can hear the banging, but upon knocking, they don't stop their stride of whatever sort of work they're doing inside with the anvil. The rain begins to let up a little bit. It's still sprinkling, but at least the heavy downpour has subsided a bit. You make your way through the slushy mud pits that the streets have become, weaving your way between horses and various wagons being pulled through, individuals that are hooded and bundled making their way around. Eventually discovering the outskirts of the Leaky Tap. As you guys step into the warm interior, pulling your hoods back, we'll pick up on that next week. We've got these guys have way too early flights to go to this convention tomorrow.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Why do you need them? I like them.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I cast spells with them. I need just a couple.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: But I like to eat them.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (Napoleon Dynamite voice) Jester, give me your tarts!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: How many tarts do I have?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You may have to come back and buy more tarts in the morning.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: If you want one. Do you want one? No, Fjord's good.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I have six left. That's okay, I have six left.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: They're really bright and garish. Really good.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can we say we go and try to sell some of this stuff, or do you guys want to do this next time?
